modeling_constraints: Do not hard-code validation stimulus, stop times, sample windows, gold internals, or simulator side channels.
problem_statement: the supplied system violates the public contract

properties.observable_contract: Drive `isup_metric` as a voltage-coded supply-current estimate. The estimate must scale with the local supply ratio `V(vdd, vss) / vnom` clipped to the range `[0, 1.5]`. Normalize load as `V(load, vss) / vhi` clipped to `[0, 1]`. When the block is disabled or powered down, meaning `V(en) <= vth` or `V(pd) > vth`, drive `isup_metric = ipd * supply_scale`. Otherwise choose the active base metric as `iq1` when `V(mode) > vth` and `iq0` when `V(mode) <= vth`, then drive `isup_metric = (base_metric + load_gain * load_norm) * supply_scale`. The metric is not a real branch current; it is an observable behavioral macro-model output.

Build a voltage-domain bias/reference/power-management macro-model metric. The module exposes an observable supply-current demand estimate as a voltage-coded output across enable, power-down, operating mode, load demand, and local supply conditions.
`vnom = 0.9 V`: nominal supply used for supply-ratio scaling.
`iq0 = 0.08`, `iq1 = 0.14`: active quiescent metric levels for low and high

title: Power Mode Supply Current Metric
